item.title: Milei Eases Grip on Argentine Peso as Reserves Hit Seven-Year High
item.description: A wave of dollars flowing into Argentina is giving President Javier Milei room to let the peso trade more freely, as the central bank’s once depleted foreign reserves have hit the highest level since 2019.

item.title: BIS and Banks Build Blockchain Cross-Border Payments Prototype
item.description: The Bank for International Settlements and a cohort of partners will soon start testing a new prototype for digital cross-border payments with real-value transactions.

item.title: India Should Ease Rules to Revive FDI, Modi’s Former Aide Says
item.description: India should liberalize rules for overseas investors by easing dispute-resolution procedures and offering tax incentives to revive foreign inflows and support economic growth, a former adviser to Prime Minister Narendra Modi said.

item.title: Dwelly in Talks to Raise $200 Million for AI Property Management
item.description: Dwelly, a UK startup that acquires real estate property managers and introduces artificial intelligence to their business, is in discussions to raise around $200 million in equity and debt financing, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Russia Boosts Crude Flows as Kremlin Banks Gains From Iran War
item.description: India’s oil imports from Russia have jumped by 70% since February

item.title: BP’s Long History of Management Turmoil Makes an Unexpected Return
item.description: The sudden firing of the oil giant’s chairman raises questions about the board’s personnel decisions.

item.title: Robinhood Launches AI Stock Trading, Purchases on Credit Cards
item.description: Robinhood Markets Inc. customers will soon be able to direct artificial intelligence agents to trade equities and make purchases on their credit cards for them.

item.title: Traders Pare BOE Rate Bets, Now Favor One 25bps Hike This Year
item.description: Traders trimmed wagers on the extent of interest rate hikes from the Bank of England this year, fueled by falling oil prices on optimism over signs of progress in talks between the US and Iran.

item.title: Brazil Inflation Breaches Top of Target Range on Food Costs
item.description: Brazil’s inflation accelerated more than expected in the first half of May, breaching the ceiling of the central bank’s target range as food and housing prices rose.

item.title: Data Center Firm PolarDC Sets Nordic Record With Junk Bond Sale
item.description: Data center operator PolarDC sold €800 million ($930 million) of high-yield bonds in the biggest deal of its kind in the Nordic market.

item.title: Copper Startup Quilla Is in IPO Talks Ahead of Peru Mining Expansion
item.description: Copper startup Quilla Resources Inc. is in discussions with prospective advisers about listing shares in Toronto as it weighs expansion plans in Peru.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 27, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.3% as of 7:35 a.m. in New York as optimism around artificial intelligence, lower oil prices and easing bond yields drives bullishness.

item.title: Adani Firms Extend Rally to Recoup Losses Sparked by Hindenburg
item.description: Shares of billionaire Gautam Adani’s group companies have erased all losses triggered by a scathing short-seller report in 2023, as the conglomerate overcomes regulatory challenges.

item.title: Makhlouf Says ECB Committed to 2% Goal, Won’t Comment on June
item.description: The European Central Bank is intent to get inflation back to its goal, Governing Council member Gabriel Makhlouf said, refusing to say if that will require an interest-rate increase next month.

item.title: Investec Raises $43 Million in Debut Loss-Absorbing Debt Issue
item.description: Investec became the latest South African bank to issue loss-absorbing debt as local lenders work to comply with the new central bank framework.

item.title: National Bank Beats on Overall Strength in Major Segments
item.description: National Bank of Canada beat analyst expectations in its fiscal second quarter on strength across its major segments while also setting aside less than anticipated for bad loans.

item.title: Airbnb and Uber Are Competing to Be the Next Big Travel Super App
item.description: Neither company appears content to lead just one category

item.title: Solid Demand to Lift Shipping Rates Underpinned by Supply Strains
item.description: Container capacity is ‘critically tight’ in Asia, allowing carriers to command higher rates

item.title: How Kevin Warsh Might Seek to Bring ‘Regime Change’ to the Fed
item.description: New Chair Kevin Warsh may start reforms with a change to the way the Fed communicates.

item.title: Guindos Says ECB Must Consider Weaker Growth at June Meeting
item.description: The European Central Bank should take a close look at how badly the Iran war is weighing on the economy when it decides whether to raise interest rates next month, according to Vice President Luis de Guindos.

item.title: US Mortgage Rates Climb to Highest Level Since August
item.description: US mortgage rates increased last week to the highest since August, restraining home-purchase activity and prompting a sharp pullback in refinancing.

item.title: Ellison Privately Vows to Keep Enlarged Paramount’s Debt in Line
item.description: It began as a concession in private conversations to assuage wary credit analysts looking at Paramount Skydance Corp.’s blockbuster takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc.: a verbal pledge by the Ellison family to do whatever it takes to slash debt at the combined company.

item.title: TSMC CEO Pledges 30%-Plus Incentive Bump as AI Profits Soar
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. chief C.C. Wei told staff they’ll see more than a 30% bump in their profit-sharing payouts this year on average, responding after some voiced concerns over their incentive plans online.

item.title: India’s Richest Civic Body Plans Nearly $1 Billion Muni Bonds
item.description: India’s richest civic authority is planning to raise as much as 95 billion rupees ($992 million) through municipal debt in one or more tranches, marking its foray into the local-currency bond market.

item.title: Temu-Owner PDD’s Revenue Misses as China Competition Persists
item.description: PDD Holding Inc.’s quarterly revenue growth missed expectations after fierce domestic competition offset an improving overseas Temu business.

item.title: Korea’s 100% Stock Run Is Rivaling Nasdaq of 1999
item.description: SK Hynix’s value tops $1 trillion and the Kospi has doubled.

item.title: More Companies Turn to Monitoring Employee AI Skills
item.description: Crypto exchange OKX joins KPMG and other firms changing performance evaluations

item.title: Warner Bros. Tightens $15 Billion Loan Price in Hot Debt Market
item.description: Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. tightened pricing on its loan offering on Wednesday, a day after upsizing the deal to around $15 billion, as investor appetite for corporate credit allows borrowers to set favorable terms.

item.title: Americans Are About to Pay Even More at the Grocery Store
item.description: As Americans confront a surge in prices at the pump, another inflation wave is headed for the grocery store.

item.title: Goldman’s Bell Says European Stocks Offer Breadth in AI Rally
item.description: European equities could attract investors who are worried about the dominance of a handful of technology stocks, according to Sharon Bell, senior European equity strategist at Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
